summary: Promote byte-parity fixtures + Rust goldens into a shared `contract/` dir both test suites read; generate schemas Rust-first (schemars → JSON Schema 2020-12, ts-rs types)

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

new.description: The shared interchange corpus both implementations test against 'one golden set' (`50-distribution.md`): what lives here (byte-parity manifest goldens, extraction goldens, the Rust-first JSON Schema + generated TS types) and the discipline that a fixture edit is an interface-version event (per chain.ts's contract/** fence note).

Rust side reads the `contract/` goldens: `write_manifest_members` output byte-matches each golden, and the extraction goldens round-trip. The single producer of the goldens the SDK suite then consumes.

edit.description: Add `schemars` (JSON Schema 2020-12 derive) and `ts-rs` (TS type generation) to the sanctioned dev/dependency set — authorized by the ratified reconcile (inbox item 2b, 'schema generation Rust-first').
Derive `schemars::JsonSchema` + `ts_rs::TS` on the manifest IR types (`ManifestMember` et al., ~line 124) so the interchange schema generates from the Rust source of truth, Rust-first.
Add a contract-schema emission path that writes the manifest/interchange JSON Schema (2020-12) into `contract/`, distinct from the existing editor-schema `emit`.
Point the byte-parity fixtures at the shared `contract/` goldens (read from disk) instead of the inline `String.raw` corpus, so both suites test one golden set.

tests.asserts: the Rust emitter's output byte-matches each `contract/` manifest golden and the generated JSON Schema is emitted deterministically; the SDK suite reads the same goldens.

acceptance: Byte-parity fixtures + Rust goldens live in `contract/` and both `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` consume that one set; the interchange JSON Schema (2020-12) + TS types generate Rust-first via schemars/ts-rs; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test + sdk test all green.
notes: Ratified reconcile, in-fence item (2b). contract/** is already in build's fence (chain.ts:209, commit 1c33491). schemars/ts-rs are new sanctioned crates authorized by the ratification. Large — build may split (schema gen vs fixture move) and raise it if a tick can't stay green. Blocked on SDK-ASSEMBLY-ARTIFACTS: it shares sdk/test/emit.test.ts and wants the emit surface stable before goldens freeze.

summary: Make `Primitive::Field` honor the nested key-path its doc-comment promises — held deferred until a nested-key kind (a JSON-manifest kind) consumes it

per.section: The extraction algebra — the soundness boundary, as data

gate.reason: no consumer — the Fenced half graduated to FENCED-PRIMITIVE (its genre-fence consumer arrived); the remaining key-path half has none. `Primitive::Field` reads a flat `fields.get(key)`, so a nested `a.b.c` path can't be read — but no current kind (skill/rule/memory) declares a nested key-path field. Revive when a kind demands it (a JSON-manifest kind's nested key-path field).

edit.description: Make `Primitive::Field` honor the key-path its own doc-comment promises: the impl does a flat `unit.frontmatter.get(key)` in `apply`, so a nested `a.b.c` path can't be read. No new vocabulary member — this fixes the existing `field` primitive.

Key-path traversal for the field extractor: walk nested TOML/JSON tables to the leaf before `json_to_feature`, kind-preserving per the `type` lattice (`specs/architecture/10-contracts.md`). Absent (not errored) when the path doesn't resolve.

tests.asserts: a key-path `field` reads a nested value and is absent (not errored) when the path doesn't resolve; it preserves the source scalar kind.

acceptance: A nested-key-path `Primitive::Field` reads `a.b.c` over a `Unit` and is absent when unresolved; a KIND.md declaring it reads the intended feature; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green. (Deferred: not built until a kind consumes it.)
notes: Narrowed 07-03: the Fenced half graduated to FENCED-PRIMITIVE (consumer arrived). Only the key-path `field` half remains, still no consumer. `Primitive::Field { key }` still flat-reads on disk (kind.rs:836). Shares kind.rs/extract.rs with AGENT-KIND — serialize the two if both un-defer.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind — `.claude/agents/*.md` as a member document, read through the generic loader and checked by the embedded `agent.anthropic` package

per.section: Decision: package binding is by artifact kind

gate.reason: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction under the requirements/satisfies reframe; and post-adapter-wave the agent kind is mostly curated data (a KIND.md + PACKAGE.md, both outside build's fence) with near-zero engine code — revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The embedded built-in `agent` KIND.md (curated — humans author it; outside build's fence), under its provider beside kinds/claude-code/skill and kinds/claude-code/rule with a `provider = "claude-code"` line: `governs = { root = '.claude/agents', glob = '*.md' }`, the declared `yaml-frontmatter` projection format + unit shape, composed extraction over the `name`/`description`/optional `tools`/`model` fields plus markdown structure. build.rs auto-embeds it — no engine extractor.

The agent kind's built-in package `agent.anthropic` (curated product territory, outside build's fence), embedded with the std-lib by build.rs. Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]`; `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores; an advisory `max_lines`. Per-clause `source` citations to the Claude Code subagent docs.

Fixture-driven proof mirroring tests/rule_contract.rs: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an import round-trip insta snapshot proving idempotence — all through the generic adapter + loader, no per-kind engine code.

edit.description: Add `"agent"` to the `BUILTIN_KINDS` name-list so a `[kind.agent]` binding reads as a built-in layer, not a custom kind.
Add the `AGENT_PACKAGE = "agent.anthropic"` const beside SKILL_PACKAGE/RULE_PACKAGE and register its kind->package binding. build.rs auto-embeds the PACKAGE.md; no manual embed edit.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the agent package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the `agent.anthropic` package (conformance) and that package passes admissibility; `temper import` projects agents as one-document members idempotently via the generic frontmatter adapter; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Declared-adapter architecture: no src/agent.rs IR — the generic adapter projects it. Curated kinds/claude-code/agent/KIND.md + packages/agent.anthropic/PACKAGE.md sit outside build's fence; engine wiring is near-zero. Stays deferred: wrong direction under requirements/satisfies. Shares src/kind.rs with EXTRACTION-VOCAB-GAPS — serialize the two if both un-defer.

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected. Distinct from the existing `.github/workflows/temper.yml` CI job.

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`, confirmed on disk). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's pattern) plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/architecture/50-distribution.md`). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering the harness manifest. Scoped/prefixed names per the resolved (project-name) fork. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (the authoring-face manifest).

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; the cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds (npm org, tap, signing). NB root `package.json` is the *private* flume manifest (≠ sdk/package.json) — reconcile or split, never blindly create a second. Only `.github/workflows/temper.yml` (CI) exists — no release.yml yet. Parked until a human sets creds up.
